rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s theoretical contrast: the citing note says prior food-choice work emphasized taste–health trade-offs, and the cited note is a mechanism paper on hedonic vs functional health attributes and consumption. Both are multi-experiment lab studies on food intake/choice mechanisms, making the citation plausibly a substantive theory/comparison source rather than an incidental mention. With this close construct overlap and a DOI-verified match, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
